dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9499 IH: Protecting Taxpayers from Ghost Preparers Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Malliotakis introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to apply tax return preparation penalties to improperly altered returns, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Taxpayers from Ghost Preparers Act.

section.header: Penalties for tax return preparers who improperly alter returns

subsection.text: Paragraph (1) of section 6696(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: any return of any tax imposed by this title,
any administrative adjustment request under section 6227,
any partnership adjustment tracking report under section 6226(b)(4)(A), and
any other document purporting to be a return, request, or report described in subparagraphs (A) through (C).

subsection.text: The amendment made by this section shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act.

section.header: Limitation period not extended for victims of preparer fraud

subsection.text: Section 6501(c)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting by the taxpayer after intent.
The amendment made by this section shall apply to assessments made or proceedings begun after the date of the enactment of this Act.

section.header: Technical amendment related to the Disaster Related Extension of Deadlines Act

subsection.text: Subsection (f) of section 7508A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by the Disaster Related Extension of Deadlines Act) is redesignated as subsection (g).
The amendment made by this subsection shall take effect as if included in section 2(a) of the Disaster Related Extension of Deadlines Act.
